title: Product Manager
jd_text:
At eBay, we're more than a global ecommerce leader — we’re changing the way the world shops and sells. Our platform empowers millions of buyers and sellers in more than 190 markets around the world. We’re committed to pushing boundaries and leaving our mark as we reinvent the future of ecommerce for enthusiasts.
Our customers are our compass, authenticity thrives, bold ideas are welcome, and everyone can bring their unique selves to work — every day. We're in this together, sustaining the future of our customers, our company, and our planet.
Join a team of passionate thinkers, innovators, and dreamers — and help us connect people and build communities to create economic opportunity for all.
The Search at eBay team is responsible for the search page that drives a large part of eBay’s core marketplace business. The Search team strives to build an experience that enables users to easily explore, find, and buy what they want at the best value through an engaging, frictionless, and trusted Search experience, helping them make confident purchase decisions.
In this role, you will be responsible to building high quality advertising solutions for Search. Your expertise will drive the development of AI-driven features that improve product discovery, engagement, and overall ad quality. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AI/ML technologies, along with a passion for applying these skills to redefine the way customers interact with our platform.
What You Will Accomplish
Lead the product strategy and roadmap for improving advertising quality on search
Be a strategic problem solver and able to synthesize data from multiple sources (including analytics, user research, marketing research, and design) to identify key customer unmet needs, key solutions that can address the needs and build product vision with search item tiles
You will work closely with a cross-functional team of engineering, design, analytics, product marketing, and user research to deliver these products
You will lead the execution of your roadmap by working with data science, engineering, and analytics teams
Build MVPs, iterate fast and be the champion of execution excellence
Conduct AB tests for launch candidates and work with analytics teams to measure the impact on success metrics
What You Will Bring
3+ years of Product Management experience
Experience with search and discovery systems, including familiarity with ranking models, search algorithms, and semantic search
Familiarity with pay per click search advertising systems
Strong analytic skills and ability to synthesize data from multiple sources
Passionate advocate for user, and has confirmed results in achieving superb user experience within tight constraints
Demonstrated leadership ability, self-motivated and directed, entrepreneurial ability to innovate quickly
Additional Details
eBay is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to race, color, religion, national origin, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, veteran status, and disability, or other legally protected status. If you have a need that requires accommodation, please contact us at talent@ebay.com. We will make every effort to respond to your request for accommodation as soon as possible. View our accessibility statement to learn more about eBay's commitment to ensuring digital accessibility for people with disabilities.
We use cookies to enhance your experience and may use AI tools for administrative tasks in the hiring process. To learn how we handle your personal data and use AI responsibly, please visit our Talent Privacy Notice, Privacy Center, and AI Hiring Guidelines.
summary: The role is for a product manager at eBay, responsible for building high-quality advertising solutions for search, driving the development of AI-driven features, and leading the product strategy and roadmap for improving advertising quality on search. The ideal candidate will have a strong background in AI/ML technologies and experience with search and discovery systems. The candidate will work closely with a cross-functional team to deliver products and lead the execution of the roadmap.

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
Product Manager (AI-Native Platform)
Location:
Berlin (Hybrid)
We're partnering with an exciting early-stage, venture-backed technology company building an AI-native platform that is transforming how complex operational workflows are managed and automated.
Backed by leading investors and operating at the forefront of AI, workflow automation and agentic systems, they are looking for a highly entrepreneurial Product Manager to join their growing team and work directly alongside the founders.
This is a unique opportunity for someone who thrives in ambiguity, enjoys speaking with customers, and wants to play a hands-on role in shaping both product strategy and execution.
The Opportunity
This is not a traditional roadmap management role.
We're looking for someone who can operate like a mini-founder; identifying customer problems, validating solutions, collaborating with engineers and designers, and driving products from idea through to launch.
You'll have significant ownership from day one and play a key role in helping shape the next generation of AI-powered workflows.
What You'll Be Doing
Product Discovery & Customer Research
Conduct customer interviews and discovery sessions
Deeply understand customer workflows, pain points and operational challenges
Translate customer insights into product opportunities
Validate assumptions before development begins
Product Strategy & Prioritisation
Work closely with founders to define product priorities
Evaluate opportunities based on customer impact and business value
Contribute to product strategy and roadmap decisions
Balance immediate customer needs with long-term product vision
Product Delivery
Collaborate closely with Engineering and Design teams
Define product requirements and acceptance criteria
Drive initiatives from concept through to launch
Ensure alignment across stakeholders and teams
AI-Native Product Development
Identify opportunities where AI can automate workflows and improve user experiences
Collaborate on AI-powered features, automation tools and agentic systems
Define success metrics and evaluate outcomes
Leverage AI tools to accelerate discovery, decision-making and execution
Analytics & Optimisation
Define and track product success metrics
Analyse user behaviour and product adoption
Identify opportunities to improve activation, engagement and retention
Drive continuous product improvements through data and customer feedback
What they are looking For
They are intentionally open-minded on background.
You may currently be:
A Product Manager
A Product Designer who has moved into Product
A Software Engineer who has transitioned into Product
A Startup Founder or Operator with strong product instincts
More important than job title is your ability to identify problems, work closely with customers and ship great products.
Must Have
3-5 years of relevant experience in Product Management, Product Design, Software Engineering or a similar role
Experience building and shipping digital products
Strong customer discovery and research skills
Excellent communication and stakeholder management abilities
Strong ownership mentality and startup mindset
Ability to operate autonomously in fast-moving environments
Experience working closely with Engineering and Design teams
We Nice to Have
B2B SaaS experience
Early-stage startup experience
Product Design background
Software Engineering background
Experience with AI products, automation platforms or workflow tools
Experience working directly with founders
German language skills
Who Will Thrive Here
Builders, not coordinators
Customer-obsessed product thinkers
Individuals who move quickly and learn through iteration
People who embrace ownership and accountability
Those who are excited by AI and emerging technologies
Strong communicators who can simplify complexity
Curious, low-ego and highly collaborative team players
Why Join?
Work directly with founders and senior leadership
Significant ownership and impact from day one
Shape an emerging AI-nati
summary: The company is seeking a Product Manager to join their team and work directly alongside the founders to shape the next generation of AI-powered workflows, with a focus on customer discovery, product strategy, and delivery. The ideal candidate will have a strong entrepreneurial mindset, excellent communication skills, and experience building and shipping digital products. This role offers significant ownership and impact from day one, with the opportunity to work on an AI-native platform that is transforming complex operational workflows.

title: Senior Product Manager
jd_text:
Immowelt is a leading German digital real estate marketplace and part of the Aviv Group, a European family of real estate platforms across Germany, France, and Belgium, building trusted property ecosystems.
THE ROLE
We are looking for a Senior Product Manager to own a key part of the Professional, agent-facing side of the Immowelt marketplace, with a specific focus on lead management, agent messaging, and our professional mobile tooling to be launched later this year.
Leads are our platform’s core value-creation activity, representing user inquiries on real estate listings.
You will own the experience determining how agents manage leads at their desks or on the move. This high-impact role involves user discovery, data-driven decision-making, and migrating agents to a modern, consolidated toolset.
You will report directly to the Product Director and work closely with a cross-functional product team. You will also play a central role in a major programme to deprecate our legacy products and migrate agents to a modern, consolidated toolset.
We need a strong mid-level PM ready to grow, offering real responsibility and a clear path to greater scope.
YOUR MISSION
Consolidate the agent workspace for managing leads by:
Executing the Agent Leads Hub roadmap for managing message enquiries and call tracking and live chat.
Leading the migration from legacy tools to modern workflows, retiring technical debt.
Scoping instant messaging and document sharing for Pro customers, get us to a go/no go decision for this functionality
Shaping the Immowelt GO mobile app (iOS/Android) featuring quality scoring, auto-responders, and chat for a late 2026 launch.
Conducting regular user discovery via site visits and data analysis to guide roadmap decisions.
WHAT YOU WILL DO
Product Discovery & Strategy
Run dual-track discovery to maintain an evidenced backlog.
Synthesise research, behavioural data, and sales feedback into a prioritised roadmap.
Define measurable goals and north-star metrics for all initiatives.
Validate opportunities through fast experimentation and iterative shipping.
Delivery & Execution
Own end-to-end delivery in partnership with engineering and design.
Engage in architecture discussions and build vs. buy decisions.
Drive legacy deprecation with clear migration plans and communication.
Ensure mobile quality across iOS and Android agent experiences.
Data & Experimentation
Define instrumentation requirements for full product measurability.
Query data independently to validate hypotheses and monitor performance.
Focus on outcome-based metrics that drive agent and business value.
Stakeholder Communication
Align stakeholders from engineering to senior leadership on strategy and progress.
Present roadmap updates to leadership using strategic context and storytelling.
Collaborate with account managers to surface pain points and close feedback loops.
WHAT WE ARE LOOKING FOR
Experience & Background
3+ years as a PM, ideally in B2B SaaS, marketplaces, or PropTech.
Proven impact from discovery through delivery in agile squads.
Experience working in cross-functional agile squads, with genuine ownership – not just backlog management.
Familiarity with dual-track discovery and delivery methodologies.
Experience with iOS/Android mobile product development is a plus.
Data & Technical Skills
Comfortable validating assumptions and monitoring product health through data.
Technical fluency to discuss APIs and delivery trade-offs with engineers.
Experience with A/B testing and validation tools.
Proficient with AI tools (Claude, ChatGPT, etc.) and prototyping (Claude Code, Replit, Lovable) is a plus.
Communication & Mindset
Structured communicator effective with technical and non-technical audiences.
Action-oriented with an ownership mindset and comfort with ambiguity.
Language & Location
Must speak & write German at B2 level or above (native speaker preferred). This is a customer-facing product role where market and linguistic understanding matters.
Strong
summary: Immowelt is seeking a Senior Product Manager to lead the development of their professional, agent-facing marketplace, focusing on lead management, agent messaging, and mobile tooling. The role involves user discovery, data-driven decision-making, and migrating agents to a modern, consolidated toolset. The ideal candidate will have experience in product management, preferably in B2B SaaS, marketplaces, or PropTech, and be able to work closely with cross-functional teams.

title: Senior Product Manager, Exchange
jd_text:
Who We Are
Cint is a pioneer in research technology (ResTech). Our customers use the Cint platform to post questions and get answers from real people to build business strategies, confidently publish research, accurately measure the impact of digital advertising, and more. The Cint platform is built on a programmatic marketplace, which is the world’s largest, with nearly 300 million respondents in over 130 countries.
At Cint, we are committed to innovation and are rapidly expanding our portfolio of data solutions to power the next generation of insights. We are looking for passionate, results-oriented leaders to join us on this journey.
Job Description
The Role
Join our Product Team as a Senior Product Manager. You’ll craft the strategic roadmap and steer the delivery of our Demand services. This role, based in the EU/UK, requires collaboration with our US teams during CET hours and can be remote or attached to one of our existing engineering offices.
We're on the hunt for skilled product managers who have a unique blend of deep technical understanding, customer obsession and a love for solving product problems. With countless potential features to bring to life in Cint, seize the opportunity to be the decision-maker on one of the most impactful domains within Cint.
What You Will Do
Own the Vision: Develop and promote a product vision for your part of Cint’s Exchange. We make long term investments with large payoffs for success, see the future and articulate it.
Tell the Story: Masterfully articulate the "why" behind your product strategy. Bring complex customer and technical problems to life, inspiring and aligning senior leadership and the broader organization around your vision.
Execute with Excellence: Conduct thorough opportunity assessments, backed by data and customer research, to validate assumptions and prioritize initiatives. Author clear, comprehensive product requirements and partner with R&D leadership to navigate complex technical trade-offs.
Partner to Win: Build strong, trusted relationships with key stakeholders across R&D, Data Science, Commercial, Operations, Legal, and Marketing to ensure cross-functional alignment and a successful go-to-market.
Qualifications
What We Are Looking For
Proven Product Operator: You have a proven track record of launching, operating and scaling complex products, platforms, or services from concept to market or transforming existing products for their next stage of growth.
Customer-Obsessed: You are an expert in translating customer problems from the ResTech and data space into clear, well-structured product requirements, backed by data and qualitative insights.
Technical Expertise: You have direct experience managing the full lifecycle of API and UI driven products and can also clearly demonstrate shipping high quality experiences. Demonstrated knowledge, experience and interest in how LLMs and modern coding stacks challenge long held verities would be a strong plus.
Exceptional Communicator: You possess outstanding written and verbal communication skills and are seen as an expert storyteller. You can effectively partner with senior leadership and are skilled at managing complex stakeholder maps.
Remote First: Ability to work independently in a remote-first environment.
Why Join Us?
Strategic Impact: You will be driving the foundation of Cint's current and future success.
Innovation: You will be on the cutting edge of ResTech and data technology, solving novel problems with a world-class R&D team.
Global Reach: Cint is a global company with a distributed, flexible, and collaborative culture.
Ownership: We empower our product team. You will have the autonomy and accountability to make a measurable impact on the business.
Additional Information
Our Values
Collaboration is our superpower
We uncover rich perspectives across the world
Success happens together
We deliver across borders.
Innovation is in our blood
We’re pioneers in our industry
Our curiosity is insatiable
We bring the
summary: Cint is seeking a Senior Product Manager to craft the strategic roadmap and steer the delivery of their Demand services, requiring collaboration with US teams and a unique blend of technical understanding, customer obsession, and problem-solving skills. The role involves developing and promoting a product vision, articulating the product strategy, and executing with excellence. The ideal candidate has a proven track record of launching and scaling complex products, is customer-obsessed, and has technical expertise in managing API and UI driven products.
preferred_skills: Experience with LLMs and modern coding stacks
Knowledge of ResTech and data technology

title: Product Manager
jd_text:
This isn’t your regular job. Almedia is a place where those who want to push harder can accelerate their careers faster than anywhere else. We’re aiming to become Germany’s second bootstrapped unicorn. Almedia is already Europe’s #3 fastest-growing company in 2025 (FT1000).
We are building the future of marketing by rewarding our community of over 70 million users for engaging with our advertisers’ products. We are offering a new way to acquire users for the biggest companies in the world.
Product Manager
€70K – €100K
Offers Equity
Freecash is the product. Almedia is the engagement engine behind it: the smartest way for mobile game studios to turn high-potential users into high-value ones. We're the leader in our category, profitable, and now expanding into new verticals to capture a much bigger market.
We're hiring a Product Manager to join the team and own real outcomes. You won't be running the whole roadmap on day one. You'll own the results of the projects you take on, have a real say in where the roadmap goes, and take on as much as you can carry. The more you can own, the more we'll hand you.
If you think like a founder, get energy from hard problems, and want to dig into data and turn it into something worth building, keep reading.
What you'll own
You won't be handed a list of tickets. You'll be handed problems and trusted to solve them.
The outcomes of your projects, not just the tasks. You own the result of the work you take on, from the problem all the way to shipped impact to millions of daily active users.
A real say in the roadmap. You bring ideas, push back, and heavily influence what we build and why.
Room to make the call. You'll own your decisions without waiting for sign-off on everything. We back conviction, and a bet that doesn't land is a lesson, not a mark against you.
As much ownership as you can carry. We hand more to the people who prove they can run with it. Show us, and your scope grows fast.
What you'll bring
You think like a founder. You operate like an owner: figuring out what matters, making the call with incomplete information, and getting it done without waiting to be told.
You live in the data. You don't ask the analyst what the chart says. You dig in yourself, follow the thread, and come back with the insight nobody else found. Hungry to get hands-on with tools like Amplitude, Hex, and Looker.
You go deep and think wide. Data tells you what to solve. Creativity decides how. You explore a few ways to crack a problem before committing, because the obvious answer is rarely the best one.
You prioritize and you ship. You pick the few things that matter right now and drive them from idea to launch. Strong bias for action.
You communicate and align. You bring developers, data, design, and business development with you without slowing down.
Impact. We don't count years in a PM title. If you spent two years building your own company and one year in product, that counts. We care about what you've built and the impact you've had.
What Puts You Ahead
These aren't requirements, but they're the patterns we keep seeing in people who thrive here.
You founded a company, and now you want to go all-in on product. This is golden to us. You know what it means to own an outcome with no one to hand it off to, and you'd rather pour that energy into building product than into anything else right now.
You've worked in a fast-moving startup. Bonus if you were there through real growth. Shifting priorities and building the plane mid-flight feel normal to you.
You want to grow fast. You're here to do the best work of your career so far, and you'll take on everything you can handle to get there.
Why Almedia? 🚀
Own Our Growth: We offer all Berlin-based employees equity in Almedia to truly be a part of our success.
Scale With Almedia: Grow alongside a startup that has been profitable from day one.
Central Berlin Office: Work from a fully-stocked modern office built for collaboration, accessible from all around Berlin.
Othe
summary: Almedia is seeking a Product Manager to own real outcomes and drive the company's growth, with a focus on building the future of marketing and rewarding a community of users for engaging with advertisers' products. The ideal candidate will think like a founder, operate with incomplete information, and drive projects from idea to launch. The company offers equity, a competitive salary, and a dynamic work environment in central Berlin.

title: Product Manager - Data-Viz Startup - B2B SaaS
jd_text:
At Vizzlo, we create tools that make data visualization accessible to everyone.
Creating high-quality, on-brand charts, visuals, and business graphics used to be hard. Vizzlo changes that. We offer the only tool on the market that integrates with Google Slides, PowerPoint, and more. Whether for presentations, business documents, or the Web—with Vizzlo, anyone can visualize their ideas in seconds.
We are a small team of enthusiasts in a SaaS startup. We’re proud of our hands-on culture, we talk plainly to each other, and improve anything we’re working on until it’s perfect.
We are looking for a hands-on and product-obsessed Product Manager to join our team remotely within Germany or in our offices in Leipzig. This is a full-time role ideal for someone who geeks out about data visualization, isn't afraid of Markdown specs and nerdy engineers, and is ready to do whatever it takes to ship an awesome product.

title: Junior Product Manager (m/w/d) Away-from-Home
jd_text:
STRONG RESULT-ORIENTATION?
PASSIONATE GASTRONOMY EXPERT?
ANALYTICAL MINDSET?
(Junior) Product Manager (m/w/d) Away-from-Home
Befristet auf 1 Jahr
Standort Frankfurt am Main (hybrid)
Unser Herz schlägt für erstklassigen Geschmack und großartige Produkte – Wir arbeiten jeden Tag gemeinsam daran, unsere Vision „No. 1 Caffè-Marke für italienischen Lifestyle“ umzusetzen.
Als (Junior) Product Manager Away-from-Home bist Du verantwortlich für Lavazza‘s Gastronomie Portfolio in Deutschland.
Der/die Positionsinhaber*in berichtet direkt an unsere Head of Portfolio und treibt die Portfolio Strategie und Produkt Aktivierung für das Außer-Haus-Geschäft voran.
Dazu gehört die Adaption der globalen Strategie auf lokale Bedürfnisse, Ableitung von Portfolio Needs und Umsetzung von Initiativen in enger Zusammenarbeit mit dem italienischen Headquarter. Ein starkes Markt- und Kanalwissen sowie Verständnis für die P&L Steuerung von Produkten sind dabei die Basis für die kontinuierliche Optimierung des Foodservice Angebots für profitables Wachstum. Mit einem Gespür für Gastronomie & Genuss, KPI-orientiertem Mindset und cross-funktionaler Stärke ist er/sie ein wichtiger Treiber für die Entwicklung des Away-from-Home Channels.
Du hast erste Produkt Management Erfahrung und Leidenschaft für Gastronomie?
Du bist auf der Suche nach einer neuen, spannenden Herausforderung?
Dann bewirb dich jetzt!
Die Stelle ist aufgrund einer Elternzeitvertretung auf 1 Jahr befristet.
Stellenbeschreibung
DICH ERWARTEN SPANNENDE AUFGABEN
Führung, Entwicklung und Aktivierung des Away-from-Home Portfolios von Lavazza in Deutschland
Kontinuierliche Portfolio Optimierung mit Fokus auf profitable Business Entwicklung, sowie Identifikation von Wachstumsmöglichkeiten basierend auf umfassenden Channel- und Marktanalysen
Strategische Marketing Planung & Umsetzung der Portfolio Maßnahmen in Zusammenarbeit mit Sales / Trade Mkt
Ergebnis orientierte Arbeit mit Segment- und Marktdaten, Ableitung von konkreten Aktionen
Enge Zusammenarbeit im cross-funktionalen Team, u.a. Content & Communications, Sales, Trade Marketing, Training Center und Headquarter, sowie externen Agenturen
Budgetplanung und -führung inkl. Rechnungsstellung in SAP & Erstellung von Reports
SO MACHST DU ALS TEAMPLAYER EINEN UNTERSCHIED
Mind. 1-2 Jahre Erfahrung in der FMCG-Branche mit Fokus auf Marketing/Produktmanagement
Erste Erfahrung mit Produkteinführungen und -änderungen in den Markt und Portfolio Entwicklung
Kenntnisse & Erfahrungen im deutschen Außer-Haus-Markt sowie der Arbeit in einer diversen Matrix-Organisation

title: Founding Product Manager
jd_text:
Why we are doing this
Hospital care in Germany is under structural strain.
Two thirds of hospitals operate at a loss, and most are stretched in staff, beds, and budgets. At the same time, modern surgery has moved on — many procedures that still mean an overnight stay no longer medically require one. The result: patients stay longer than they need to, infrastructure carries avoidable load, and the system absorbs costs it could avoid.
We're building a new generation of ambulatory surgery centres in Germany to change this.
Software-driven from day one. Designed to get people back to their lives faster, and to make outpatient surgery the default — not the exception.
Healthcare is a complex space. We are set up to make a difference:
Our leadership team has built and scaled 5+ successful ventures; we are backed by top tech and healthcare investors; and with multiple clinical partners onboard we are ready to move fast.
What you’ll do
You take product ownership of TokuraOS
—
the “orchestration engine” that sits on top of existing clinic stacks, orchestrating the entire care pathway end to end
, from patient intake to surgery, recovery, and daily operations, connecting digital patient journeys, on-site infrastructure, and a unified data layer into a production-grade platform.
Greenfield. Built to work when a surgeon is standing in the operating room. Built to scale across 100+ clinics.
What the role offers
First product hire.
Clear path to building, shaping and leading the product organisation alongside our CPTO. A rare opportunity to define a mission-critical product from ground-up.
Real ownership from day 1.
You shape the vision, define the roadmap, own how the product behaves in real clinic environments, and drive the end user change management. You'll split your time between product development — deciding what we build, for whom, and why it matters — and hands-on field research in and out of the OR.
Strategic freedom by design.
We have a strong hypothesis on what we need to build when, but the final product roadmap is intentionally open, to be co-shaped with you.
“Full-stack” in the truest sense.
We're not
just
building a technical product — we're building and operating physical clinics; harder than a pure software play, but far more impactful.
Small, top-tier, high-trust team.
You’ll work side by side with our CPTO (serial tech founder (YCombinator), ex-McK) and an experienced founder-duo that has built and scaled multiple hyper-growth startups to 400+ employees.
What you bring
Essential
You've shaped products with meaningful usage in complex, real-world environments.
The ability to translate the messy reality of a care journey into an experience that feels
genuinely
different — for patients, providers, and the broader care team. From intake to post-op recovery: every touchpoint is yours to understand deeply and shape optimally.
You are a strategic thinker, bring structure to any analysis, have an eye for design. You are comfortable in the space where product meets tech.
A strong pull toward learning. Trajectory matters more than credentials and experience.
Structured thinking, clear communication. You build trust quickly, whether you're with a surgeon, a nurse, or a patient.
High ownership, reliability, and a bias toward shipping with fast feedback loops.
Bonus
Prior experience working with sensitive data and/ or in regulated environments where compliance and data protection are design constraints, not afterthoughts.
Healthcare/ healthtech background.
Logistics
Berlin. On-site. Start ASAP.
Attractive compensation with meaningful equity upside.
Professional German required.
We value judgment over process, clarity over complexity, and systems that work over systems that impress.
We believe the best products emerge when everyone understands the real environments their work serves and takes ownership of what we design, build, and ship.
If this resonates, let’s talk.
summary: The role is for a product owner to lead the development of TokuraOS, an orchestration engine for ambulatory surgery centers, with a focus on shaping the product vision, defining the roadmap, and driving user change management. The ideal candidate will have experience in shaping products for complex, real-world environments and be able to translate care journeys into optimal user experiences. The company is a healthcare startup with a strong leadership team and backing from top tech and healthcare investors, offering a clear path to building and leading the product organization.

title: Product Manager - Growth (m/w/d)
jd_text:
keleya is an innovative FemTech startup based in Berlin that offers expectant and new parents high-quality support and resources to help them enjoy a healthy and happy pregnancy, birth, and parental leave.
Our mobile app is unique in its combination of a personalized pregnancy calendar, a health tracker, and a selection of workouts, recipes, and a midwife chat for expectant and new mothers—much of it covered by health insurance at no cost to endusers. Since our founding in summer 2017, we have been working on our mission with passion, agility, and team spirit and have already supported over 1.000,000 women.
The Role:
We're looking for a Growth PM to own and accelerate our user acquisition and conversion funnels, from first touchpoint to in-app subscription.
At keleya, growth is powered by a B2B2C model: we partner with health insurers who offer our app as a covered benefit, so our success depends on driving engagement that users love and insurers can stand behind. You'll lead a cross-functional squad and work closely with marketing and business development to hit our growth targets.
Tasks
Run experiments across the full funnel — from top-of-funnel awareness to in-app subscription conversion — and own the growth product roadmap, metrics (activation, retention, conversion), and the prioritisation calls that come with it.
Work closely with engineering to ship experiments and features that move the numbers.
Partner with marketing and business development to align campaigns, messaging, and health insurance partnerships with growth targets.
Identify where acquisition campaigns convert (or don't) in-app, and use those insights to inform product decisions.
Requirements
3+ years of experience as a PM, ideally in a mobile app.
Solid understanding of growth levers — paid, organic, and product-led.
Strong analytical instinct: you can trace a drop-off back to a cause and turn it into a testable hypothesis.
Comfortable using AI tools to move faster and work smarter.
A clear communicator who can work across product, engineering, marketing, and business development.
Working proficiency in English; German is a plus.
Based in Berlin.
Benefits
Meaningful work in digital health, with real impact on women's lives at a critical moment.
Real ownership and responsibility in a fast-growing FemTech startup.
Hybrid setup: minimum 2 days/week in our office in Berlin-Mitte, with up to 3 months fully remote per year.
Urban Sports Club or Pluxee membership.
summary: keleya is seeking a Growth PM to lead user acquisition and conversion funnels, working closely with marketing and business development to drive growth, with a focus on a B2B2C model and health insurance partnerships. The ideal candidate will have 3+ years of experience as a PM, a solid understanding of growth levers, and strong analytical instincts. The role offers meaningful work in digital health, with real ownership and responsibility in a fast-growing FemTech startup.

title: Group Product Manager - (Logistics, Payments and Incentives)
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a
Group Product Manager - (Logistics, Payments and Incentives)
to join the
Logistics Rider Product
on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.
In our
Logistics Team,
you’ll tackle high-impact challenges that make last-mile delivery efficient, affordable, and sustainable. Your work will directly improve experiences for riders, end customers, and merchants across the globe. Each enhancement you contribute will help Delivery Hero optimize delivery operations, supporting expansion into new areas like grocery and retail.
Payments & Incentives are core to how Delivery Hero operates at scale. Every day, our logistics platform connects vendors, riders, and customers across multiple verticals such as food and groceries — making thousands of real-time decisions to balance speed, cost, and reliability.
In this role, you’ll guide a group of five Product Managers, each owning a critical part of our rider logistics systems including topics such as Rider payments calculation, payroll & settlement systems, Algo based payments and incentive products. Together, you’ll shape how our earning systems evolve and scale across markets and use cases.
This role is a great fit for someone who enjoys working on complex, technical problem spaces, collaborating closely with engineers and data scientists, and leading experienced PMs — even if the work isn’t always customer-facing.
Own critical projects simultaneously and OKRs for key initiatives contributing directly to the success of the company.
Own the overall product direction, priorities and performance for Payments & Incentives.
Lead, support, and develop five Product Managers working with Rider calculations, settlements, automation, incentives and rider performance related products.
Create alignment across teams while giving individual PMs the autonomy to own their problem spaces. Help teams make thoughtful trade-offs between delivery time, cost efficiency, rider experience, and system reliability.
Hiring and coaching product managers and indirect reports within the domain.
Guide how new features are rolled out safely across markets, including experimentation and guardrails.
Work closely with engineering, data science, and operations partners to align on goals and execution.
Act as the main product contact for senior stakeholders, including Tech M2s, data leads, and operations teams. Communicate progress, impact, and risks clearly to Director and VP-level stakeholders.
Qualifications
8+ years of experience in experience in product management and 4+ years of experience as a people manager
Experience with payment backend, payment algorithms, and customer facing earnings management products.
Previous experience in conceptualizing B2B product solutions, engaging with business and/or strong regional stakeholders
Track record of successfully launching and constantly improving B2B products
Experience in managing a diverse set of stakeholders from around the world
Ability to balance customer needs with business priorities and a mindset for data-driven decision making
Strong written and verbal communication skills; ability to explain complex concepts in simple words
Additional Information
Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.

The ideal candidate will have a track record of launching and improving B2B products, with experience in payment backend, algorithms, and customer-facing earnings management products.

title: Senior Product Manager
jd_text:
Superhuman offers a dynamic hybrid working model for this role. This flexible approach gives team members the best of both worlds: plenty of focus time along with in-person collaboration that helps foster trust, innovation, and a strong team culture.
About Superhuman
Grammarly is now part of Superhuman, the AI productivity platform on a mission to unlock the superhuman potential in everyone. The Superhuman suite of apps and agents brings AI wherever people work, integrating with over 1 million applications and websites. The company’s products include Grammarly’s writing assistance, Coda’s collaborative workspaces, Mail’s inbox management, and Go, the proactive AI assistant that understands context and delivers help automatically. Founded in 2009, Superhuman empowers over 40 million people, 50,000 organizations, and 3,000 educational institutions worldwide to eliminate busywork and focus on what matters. Learn more at superhuman.com.
The Opportunity
We’re looking for a Senior Growth Product Manager to join our Growth team in Europe and help millions more people discover, try, and adopt Superhuman’s suite of products.
Growth at Superhuman is a product discipline. You’ll own key parts of the user journey—from first visit through signup, activation, and long-term engagement—and you’ll move growth metrics by shipping experiments, optimizing funnels/loops, and building product experiences that help users find value faster. You’ll work with a dedicated engineering team in the EU and collaborate closely with design, data science, and marketing to turn insights into impact.
This is a role for someone who is energized by experimentation, comfortable with ambiguity, and motivated by measurable outcomes.
In This Role, You Will
Own and improve conversion across key stages of the user funnel—from visitor to signup, signup to activated user, and activated to fully engaged user
Identify growth opportunities through quantitative analysis and qualitative research, then prioritize based on expected impact
Design and run a rigorous experimentation program: form hypotheses, define success metrics, ship tests, analyze results, and iterate
Optimize onboarding, signup flows, landing pages, and in-product experiences to accelerate time-to-value for new users
Collaborate with engineering to scope, build, and ship growth features at a high cadence
Partner with data science to build dashboards, define growth metrics, and develop a deep understanding of user behavior across the funnel
Work cross-functionally with marketing, product, and design teams to align growth initiatives with broader product and business goals
Share learnings and build institutional knowledge around what works, contributing to a strong experimentation culture across the organization
Qualifications
Has 5+ years of product management experience, with at least 2 years focused on growth, funnel optimization, or experimentation in a consumer or SaaS product
Track record of improving growth metrics through experimentation—can point to specific results from tests they designed and shipped
Strong analytical capabilities: comfortable working with large datasets, building analyses, and using data to inform product decisions. Proficiency with SQL or a similar query language is an advantage
Deep understanding of the user acquisition, activation, and conversion funnels—knows how to diagnose drop-offs, form hypotheses, and validate solutions
Experience running a structured experimentation program, including A/B testing, and iterative learning
Effective collaborator who builds trust with engineering, design, data science, and marketing partners
Clear and concise communicator who can articulate strategy, trade-offs, and results to both technical and non-technical audiences
Comfortable operating with ambiguity and shifting priorities—can structure problems independently and move forward without waiting for perfect information
Has a demonstrated ability to work independently with minimal guidance, pr

title: Founding Product Manager - KI-Agenten-Plattform (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Das Problem
Mittelständische Unternehmen treffen jeden Tag hunderte kleine Entscheidungen. Freigaben, Zuordnungen, Nachverfolgungen. Einzeln nicht komplex, aber in Summe fressen sie ganze Arbeitstage. Klassische Automatisierung hilft nur bei dem, was regelbasiert ist. Der schwierige Teil ist das menschliche Urteilsvermögen.
Wir bauen eine eigene Plattform, die genau dieses Problem löst: mit KI-Agenten, die operative Entscheidungen eigenständig übernehmen können. Wir wollen KI in den Mittelstand bringen, die Grenzen des Möglichen ausreizen und deutsche Unternehmen zukunftsfähig machen. Wir arbeiten direkt mit Unternehmern, die ihr Geschäft ernst meinen.
Das Produkt existiert, wird aktiv bei zahlenden Kunden eingesetzt und weiterentwickelt. Jetzt suchen wir die Person, die daraus ein eigenständig skalierendes Produkt macht. Als erster Produkthire.
Aufgaben
Die Rolle
Wir sind an dem Punkt, an dem aus einzelnen Kundenlösungen ein eigenständiges Produkt werden will. Diese Rolle entscheidet mit, wie das aussieht.
Wir sind ein kleines Team. Keine großen Abstimmungsschleifen, kein Ticket-Pingpong. Du bekommst Kontext, triffst Entscheidungen und siehst den Impact Deiner Arbeit innerhalb von Tagen.
Marek hat Lucid Labs gegründet, vorher Mitgründer von Flightright (0 auf 200+ Mitarbeiter, einer der erfolgreichsten Legal-Tech-Ventures Europas). Kurze Wege zu allen Entscheidungen, viel Eigenverantwortung. Du gestaltest, wie Produkt bei Lucid Labs funktioniert.
Die zwei Hälften
60% Customer Development.
Unsere zahlenden Kunden sind gleichzeitig unser Discovery-Engine. Du führst die Beziehungen zu 4–6 strategischen Design Partnern auf CEO/COO-Ebene. Nicht als Delivery Manager, sondern als PM, der Produktthesen direkt am lebenden Objekt validiert. Engineering steuerst Du über klare Outcomes, nicht über Tickets. Unsere Agenten liefern bei Kunden heute schon messbare Einsparungen im sechsstelligen Bereich pro Jahr.
40% Produktstrategie & Plattformaufbau.
Du verantwortest, was aus der Plattform wird: Positionierung, Featureschnitte, Verpackung. Du baust den Backlog, beobachtest den Wettbewerb, entwickelst alle zwei Wochen neue Demos und entscheidest, welche Einzellösung ein Modul wird und welche nicht.
Die zentrale Frage: Welche Features schaffen echten Wert? Wie wird aus einzelnen Kundenanforderungen ein skalierbares Produkt?
Unser Modell
Wir bauen tiefe Implementierung beim Kunden und eigenes Produkt. Diese Kombination ist unser Vorsprung: Was wir bei Kunden lernen, fließt direkt in die Plattform. Was wir an der Plattform bauen, macht uns beim nächsten Kunden besser.
Wie groß das in 24 Monaten wird, hängt davon ab, was wir gemeinsam daraus machen. Klar ist: Die Plattform wächst aus echten Kundenproblemen, nicht aus Strategie-Workshops.
Wir suchen Leute, die Lust haben, diese Reise mitzugestalten.
Wie wir Produkt bauen
Wir starten nicht mit einer Idee und validieren sie irgendwann. Wir starten beim Kunden, in echten Projekten. Wir sehen, wo es hakt. Wir bauen eine Lösung. Dann schauen wir: Taucht das gleiche Problem beim nächsten Kunden wieder auf? Wenn ja, wird aus der Einzellösung ein Feature. Aus dem Feature ein Modul. Aus dem Modul ein Teil der Plattform.
Realität, Lösung, Impact, Skalierung.
Beispiel: Letzte Woche merken wir bei einer Workshop-Vorbereitung, dass ein Teil unserer Methodik nicht weit genug trägt. Mittwoch, 3 Stunden Arbeit: Neue Version gebaut. Donnerstagmorgen, 9 Uhr: Läuft live beim Kunden. Im Workshop merken wir, wo es noch hakt. Am nächsten Tag ist die nächste Iteration fertig.
Der Abstand zwischen Idee, Umsetzung und Validierung beträgt bei uns Tage, nicht Monate.
Die schwierige Frage ist dabei immer dieselbe: Ist das, was wir gebaut haben, ein cleverer Hack für einen Kunden, oder der Kern von etwas, das für 50 Kunden funktioniert? Diese Entscheidung richtig zu treffen, ist Kern des Jobs.
Qualifikation
Was Du mitbringst
Du hast schon mindestens einmal ein B2B-Produkt von null auf echte Traction gebracht. Als Founding PM

title: Group Product Manager, DMarts - Quick Commerce
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a
Group Product Manager
to join the Quick Commerce team and be part of our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.
Be part of redefining how customers experience quick commerce. You’ll help build technology that scales our non-food offerings, reaching new market segments and driving revenue growth. By innovating within our Quick Commerce Team, you’ll make Delivery Hero the go-to platform for a broad range of products, helping us grow faster and deliver more value to customers around the world.
In our Dmart team, you’ll tackle high-impact challenges that make the operations of our micro fulfillment centers (Dmarts) more efficient. Your work will directly improve experiences for pickers, store managers, customers, and suppliers across the globe. Each enhancement you contribute will help Delivery Hero optimize operations and commercial aspects of our retail business.
The Dmart Tools domain owns the core of our Dmart operations and covers the most critical areas like: inbounding, inventory management, store & task management and pick and pack.
You will lead a team of 3 to 4 Product Managers and co-lead an organization of :30 people together with Engineering and Design counterparts. In this role, you will directly influence key business outcomes such as seamless fulfillment, cost per order, utilization rate, orders delivered on time and more.
Lead, coach, and develop 3 to 4 Product Managers, setting a clear vision and driving accountability for impactful product outcomes
Define and execute the strategy and roadmap for Dmart Operations Tools across the different key areas
Partner with Engineering and Design leadership to co-lead a :30-person organization, fostering collaboration, innovation, and delivery excellence
Drive measurable business impact by improving operational efficiency in the stores and fulfillment speed & accuracy for the customer
Collaborate closely with stakeholders across operations, commercial, analytics, product domains, and multiple Brands to ensure alignment between global scale and local market needs
Qualifications
7+ years of product management experience, including 2+ years leading Product Managers
Strong track record of delivering impact in internal and/or SaaS tools
Excellent communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to align priorities and build strong relationships across multiple teams and functions
Technical acumen to engage deeply on app architecture, design systems, and integrity solutions
Analytical and outcome-oriented, with experience leading business-critical KPIs
Outstanding communication and leadership skills to inspire teams and drive cross-functional alignment
Additional Information
Ensuring you and all our Heroes are looked after, happy, and healthy is always on the menu. Because if you’re in good shape, then we’re in good shape.
Make the most of our hybrid working model and join the team for face-to-face connection and collaboration in our beautiful Berlin campus 2 days a week
We offer 27 days holiday with an extra day on 2nd and 3rd year of service
We will support you in developing yourself and your career growth opportunities: 1.000 € Educational Budget, Language Courses, Parental Support and access to the Udemy Business platform to explore a variety of online courses.
Get moving and release tho

title: Senior Technical Product Manager
jd_text:
This position is listed on behalf of a partner company, who manages all applications and next steps. Our partner is looking for a Senior Technical Product Manager based in Germany.
As a Senior Technical Product Manager, you will drive the vision, strategy, and evolution of a highly technical platform at the intersection of software engineering, AI, and enterprise technology. This role is ideal for a former engineer who enjoys remaining hands-on while shaping product direction and delivering meaningful business outcomes. You will work closely with engineering, data science, and business teams to create scalable solutions that solve complex operational challenges for technical users. From experimenting with APIs and AI models to defining long-term platform strategy, you will play a pivotal role in influencing both product innovation and customer success. Operating within a collaborative and globally distributed environment, you will have the opportunity to directly impact product quality, developer experience, and platform adoption across international markets.
Accountabilities
Own and execute the product vision, strategy, and roadmap for a core technical platform, balancing immediate delivery priorities with long-term architectural objectives.
Actively use and validate platform capabilities through hands-on experimentation, testing APIs, deploying environments, identifying issues, and improving the overall user experience.
Build prototypes, proofs of concept, and AI-driven solutions to evaluate new opportunities and validate product ideas before development investment.
Develop and maintain reference applications, technical demonstrations, and infrastructure templates that support product adoption and platform reliability.
Define product requirements, success metrics, technical scopes, and priorities in collaboration with engineering and technical leadership teams.
Conduct ongoing research and user discovery with developers, data engineers, DevOps professionals, and other technical stakeholders to inform roadmap decisions.
Coordinate cross-functional initiatives involving engineering, data science, sales, and marketing teams to ensure successful product delivery.
Champion developer experience by identifying and addressing friction points related to integrations, deployment workflows, platform usability, and documentation.
Represent the product externally through technical communities, customer engagements, and industry discussions when appropriate.
Requirements
Previous professional experience as a software engineer, with hands-on knowledge of modern programming languages such as Python, TypeScript, or similar technologies.
Minimum of 5 years of experience in technical product management, product leadership, or a comparable role within highly technical environments such as AI platforms, data products, cloud infrastructure, or B2B SaaS solutions.
Strong technical understanding of software development lifecycles, APIs, cloud environments, platform architecture, and developer tooling.
Experience building prototypes, evaluating emerging technologies, and working with AI, machine learning, large language models, or AI-assisted development workflows.
Proven ability to translate complex technical challenges into clear product strategies, roadmaps, requirements, and actionable deliverables.
Exceptional communication and stakeholder management skills, with the ability to engage effectively with both technical teams and executive audiences.
Strong analytical mindset and problem-solving capabilities, with a passion for improving product quality and user experience.
Comfortable working in fast-paced, globally distributed teams while managing multiple priorities and cross-functional initiatives.
Willingness and ability to travel regularly across the EMEA region to collaborate with customers and stakeholders on-site.
High level of ownership, curiosity, adaptability, and enthusiasm for emerging technologies and innovation.
Benefits
Opportunity

title: Product Manager - New Products (m/w/d)
jd_text:
Deine Rolle bei Ocumeda
Ocumeda entwickelt sich von einem Produkt zu einer Multi-Product Company. Als Product Manager - New Products verantwortest du ob und wie neue Produktideen für Ocumeda funktionieren können, von der ersten Hypothese bis in die Markteinführung. Du bist Teil des Product Teams und arbeitest eng mit dem Product Lead, den bestehenden PMs, unserem Medical Team und den Entwicklern zusammen. Wenn du Lust hast in einem dynamischen Umfeld etwas aufzubauen und offen bist für eine Rolle die sich entwickelt, bist du bei uns richtig.
Du verantwortest neue Produktideen von der ersten Hypothese bis zum validierten Konzept und treibst sie strukturiert und nah an echten Nutzern voran.
Du führst Discovery Prozesse durch: Nutzerinterviews, Gespräche mit Experten, Marktanalysen und Wettbewerbsvergleiche.
Du baust Prototypen oder einfache MVPs um Kernannahmen zu validieren bevor wir in Entwicklung investieren.
Du denkst den Business Case mit: Marktpotenzial, Skalierbarkeit über mehrere Märkte und Länder, Preisstrategie und Ressourcenbedarf.
Du begleitest jedes Produkt durch Pilots, misst ob die Kernannahmen stimmen und bereitest auf Basis der Ergebnisse die Entscheidung über den weiteren Rollout vor.
Das bringst du mit
Du hast ca. 3 bis 7 Jahre Erfahrung als Product Manager, idealerweise in frühen Produktphasen oder in einem Startup bzw. Scale-up Umfeld.
Du weißt wie man Hypothesen formuliert, die richtigen Fragen stellt und schnell lernt ob eine Idee trägt.
Du hast Erfahrung mit Nutzerforschung undPrototypingTools und weißt wie man schnell lernt.
Du kannst mit Stakeholdern umgehen und komplexe (medizinische) Themen in klare Produktanforderungen übersetzen.
Du kommunizierst klar und strukturiert auf Deutsch und Englisch.
Erfahrung im Medtech oder Healthcare Umfeld sowie mit einem SaaS Produkt ist ein Plus.
Was dich bei uns erwartet
Flexible Arbeitszeiten und hybrides Arbeiten mit Homeoffice-Möglichkeit
Vergünstigtes Deutschlandticket für Mitarbeitende
Persönliches Weiterbildungsbudget für Trainings, Konferenzen und fachliche Weiterentwicklung
Viel Eigenverantwortung, schnelle Entscheidungen und echte Mitgestaltungsmöglichkeiten
Regelmäßiges Feedback, kurze Entscheidungswege und individuelle Weiterentwicklungsmöglichkeiten
Internationales Team mit enger Zusammenarbeit, regelmäßigem Austausch und gemeinsamen Teamevents
Zusammenarbeit mit Expert:innen aus Augenoptik, Augenheilkunde, Software- & KI-Entwicklung sowie Strategieberatung
Monatlicher Zuschuss zur Wellpass-Mitgliedschaft
Wettbewerbsfähiges Gehalt passend zu deiner Rolle und Erfahrung
Modernes,dog-friendly Office im Herzen Münchens mit direkter Nähe zum Hauptbahnhof
Mitarbeit an einer führenden Teleophthalmologie-Plattform, die den Zugang zur augenärztlichen Versorgung nachhaltig verbessert
Etabliertes B2B2C-Modell mit über 800 Partner-Optikerstandorten und mehr als 500.000 durchgeführten Screenings
Über uns
Ocumeda
ist ein Teleophthalmologie-Scale-up, das niederschwellige Augen-Check-Ups in Zusammenarbeit mit Optiker:innen anbietet.
Wir verhindern Sehverlust und Erblindung
durch einfache und schnelle Augen-Check-Ups. Durch unsere hochstandardisierte teleophthalmologische Auswertung bieten wir bestmögliche medizinische Qualität - unabhängig vom Ort.
Auf unser Team sind wir besonders stolz.
Wir sind interdisziplinär mit höchstem medizinischem Anspruch. Gemeinsam arbeiten wir an unserer Vision, unnötige Erblindungen zu vermeiden. Dir ist eine sinnstiftende Arbeit in einem interdisziplinären Team wichtig? Wir freuen uns von dir zu hören.
summary: The role of Product Manager - New Products at Ocumeda involves developing and launching new product ideas from hypothesis to market launch, working closely with the product team, medical team, and developers. The company offers a dynamic work environment, flexible working hours, and opportunities for personal growth and development.

title: Product Manager, Regulatory Outputs & Product Intelligence
jd_text:

About Worldly
Worldly is the world’s most comprehensive impact intelligence platform — delivering real data to businesses on impacts within their supply chain. Worldly is trusted by 40,000 global brands, retailers, and manufacturers to provide the single source of ESG intelligence they need to accelerate business and industry transformation.
Through strategic and meaningful customer relationships, Worldly provides key insights around supplier performance, product impact, trends analysis, and compliance. When a company wants to change how business is done, we enable that systemic shift.
Backed by a dedicated global team of individuals aligned by values, Worldly proudly operates as a public benefit corporation with backing from mission-aligned investors. Want to learn more? Read our story.
About The Opportunity
The Product Manager, Regulatory Outputs & Product Intelligence owns the product areas that sit at the intersection of product data, environmental methodology, and EU regulatory compliance: Product Impact Calculator (PIC), Material Sustainability Index (MSI), Product Environmental Footprint (PEF), and Digital Product Passport (DPP). These are among the most externally-visible and regulation-driven areas of the Worldly platform, operating in a competitive landscape that is moving fast.
This role is as much market-facing as it is product-building. You’ll spend meaningful time with customers and prospects, own our competitive intelligence in this space, and serve as the internal authority on how the regulatory environment translates into product decisions. You’ll work closely with Sustainability Manager and Product Design personas, and partner with GTM on commercial conversations where PIC and DPP are the deciding factor.
What You’ll Do
Own the full product strategy and roadmap for Worldly’s product tools suite, including PIC, MSI, and Regulatory Outputs (PEF, DPP, EcoScore)
Drive market and competitive intelligence for the product tools space, including DPP container providers, LCA platforms, PEF specialists, and EU-focused competitors
Lead ongoing customer discovery through regular direct engagement with brand customers and prospects; represent Product in commercial conversations where PIC, DPP, and PEF are in scope
Track the regulatory environment — ESPR delegated act developments, PEFCR updates, and national regulations including France Decree 2025-957 — and translate findings into clear product implications and roadmap adjustments
Identify and scope data partnerships for EF database access ahead of EF 4.0
Operate as a hybrid inbound/outbound PM: pull signals from customers and the market and push them into roadmap and GTM planning in equal measure
Partner cross-functionally with Sustainability, Product Design, and GTM teams to align on priorities and drive execution
We’d Like To See
Experience in textile, soft good or hard goods product development: how products are designed, developed and sourced by consumer goods brands. You understand the supply chain and how it works, and what both brands and suppliers need out of it.
5+ years of product management experience; B2B SaaS required; supply chain, sustainability, and/or climate tech strongly preferred.
Ideally you have direct, hands-on experience with LCA, PEF, product carbon footprinting, or environmental data methodologies — enough depth to hold a credible conversation with a sustainability director or LCA practitioner.
Genuine comfort being customer-facing — you should want to be in those conversations, not just tolerating them
Demonstrated ability to track a complex, evolving regulatory environment and make clear product decisions from ambiguous signals
Experience conducting competitive intelligence and market analysis, not just consuming it
A track record of working effectively across product, commercial, and technical teams
It Helps If You have
Familiarity with EU regulatory frameworks, par

At Vizzlo, we create tools that make data visualization accessible to everyone.
Creating high-quality, on-brand charts, visuals, and business graphics used to be hard. Vizzlo changes that. We offer the only tool on the market that integrates with Google Slides, PowerPoint, and more. Whether for presentations, business documents, or the Web—with Vizzlo, anyone can visualize their ideas in seconds.
We are a small team of enthusiasts in a SaaS startup. We’re proud of our hands-on culture, we talk plainly to each other, and improve anything we’re working on until it’s perfect.
We are looking for a hands-on and product-obsessed
Product Manager
to join our team remotely within Germany or in our offices in Leipzig. This is a full-time role ideal for someone who geeks out about data visualization, isn't afraid of Markdown specs and nerdy engineers, and is ready to do whatever it takes to ship an awesome product.
Tasks
As our Product Manager, you own the "what" and the "why" behind everything we ship. You talk to customers, dig through usage data, write crisp specs, and sit down with our engineers to turn ideas into features people love. In a small team, no task is "not your job"—and you wouldn't have it any other way.
Your responsibilities are:
Product Roadmap
: Define and prioritize what we build next based on customer feedback, market insights, and business goals.
Specs & Documentation
: Write clear, detailed specs in Markdown—because that's how we roll—so engineering knows exactly what to build and why.
Customer Discovery
: Talk to customers. A lot. Understand their workflows, pain points, and what "mind-blowing" means to them.
Cross-Team Collaboration
: Work
daily
with engineering and design to ship iteratively and fast.
AI Exploration
: Evaluate where AI can genuinely make our product smarter—and where it's just hype.
Data-Driven Decisions
: Track product metrics, run experiments, and use data to validate ideas (or kill your darlings).
Get Shit Done
: Do whatever it takes to move the product forward—whether that's coordinating a launch, triaging a bug, answering a support ticket, or making coffee for the team.
Requirements
Excellent written and spoken English skills
(a must); Good German skills are a plus.
2+ years of experience in product management, ideally in a SaaS or B2B environment.
A genuine interest in data visualization, charts, and making complex information look great.
The ability to write specs that engineers actually enjoy reading. Markdown fluency is a must.
No fear of working closely with nerdy engineers—in fact, you consider yourself one of them.
Curiosity about AI/ML and a pragmatic sense for where it can improve a product.
A bias for action: you'd rather ship something imperfect and iterate than debate for weeks.
Bonus: you've used R, PowerBI, Tableau, D3.js, played with data viz tools, or have opinions about bar charts vs. pie charts.
Benefits
Full-time position with a competitive remuneration package.
Start-up environment where your talents and motivation can make a real impact.
International team of highly motivated doers.
Fully remote position (within Germany), with ultra-nice offices in Leipzig (Plagwitz) for optional in-person collaboration.
Need something else? Ask!
summary: Vizzlo is seeking a hands-on and product-obsessed Product Manager to join their team remotely within Germany or in their offices in Leipzig, responsible for defining and prioritizing the product roadmap, writing specs, and collaborating with engineers and designers. The ideal candidate has excellent written and spoken English skills, 2+ years of experience in product management, and a genuine interest in data visualization. The role involves working closely with nerdy engineers, evaluating AI opportunities, and making data-driven decisions to ship an awesome product.

title: Senior Technical Product Manager, Agentic AI IDEs (Developer Tools)-Tech Foundations
jd_text:
As the world’s pioneering local delivery platform, our mission is to deliver an amazing experience, fast, easy, and to your door. We operate in around 65 countries worldwide powered by tech, designed by people. As one of Europe’s largest tech platforms, headquartered in Berlin, Germany. Delivery Hero has been listed on the Frankfurt Stock Exchange since 2017 and is part of the MDAX stock market index. We enable creative minds to deliver solutions that create impact within our ecosystem. We move fast, take action and adapt. No matter where you're from or what you believe in, we build, we deliver, we lead. We are Delivery Hero.
Job Description
We are on the lookout for a
Senior Technical Product Manager, to join the Agentic AI Developer Tools team
within Tech foundations on our journey to always deliver amazing experiences.
As a member of this team, you’ll support Delivery Hero’s rapid innovation and growth. You’ll work on foundational systems that enable faster feature development, security, and reliability across our global engineering community. Every enhancement you make will contribute to our teams' ability to build, scale, and deliver quality features—ultimately impacting millions of users worldwide.
As a PM in the Tech Foundation Team, you will be the bridge between cutting-edge AI capabilities and the daily workflows of Delivery Hero’s global engineering community. You won’t just be rolling out tools; you will be defining the future of how software is built at scale. Your mission is to transform the "Agentic IDE" from a technical concept into a high-adoption product that fundamentally changes our engineering velocity.
The Agentic IDEs team is central to the Agentic Engineering focus area, which has the bold Northstar of achieving 100% daily AI-usage of engineers in 2026.
What you will do
Product Strategy & Roadmap: Own the product vision for Agentic IDEs and CLIs. You will define the roadmap that transitions our global engineering force from "AI-assisted coding" to "AI-guided feature generation."
User Research & Empathy: Deep-dive into the developer experience (DevEx). Conduct interviews and surveys across our global entities (eFood, Glovo, Talabat) to understand friction points and identify opportunities for agentic automation.
Measurement & ROI: Define and track the success metrics for AI adoption. You will move beyond "vanity metrics" to measure true impact on PR velocity, Time-to-Market, and the Human-to-AI code ratio.
Contextual Integration Strategy: Partner with engineering to prioritize which internal data sources (APIs, Engineering Manifesto, Slack, GDrive) provide the highest leverage when integrated into the agentic ecosystem.
Go-to-Market (GTM) & Internal Adoption: Treat the IDE rollout like a product launch. Develop upskilling programs, documentation, and feedback loops to ensure the tool isn't just "available," but "essential" to every engineer’s workflow.
Stakeholder Management: Align with tech leadership across the group to ensure the agentic stack meets the security, reliability, and scalability standards of a distributed global ecosystem.
Qualifications
What you need to be successful
To be successful in this role, you should bring a blend of product discovery frameworks, a technical mindset, and the influence required to drive behavioral change across a massive organization.
Developer-as-a-Customer: Experience in Product Management, ideally within Developer Tools, Internal Platforms, or B2B SaaS.
Data-Informed Decision Making: Ability to use Northstar metrics to prioritize a backlog and say "no" to features that don't drive core adoption goals.
Modern Dev Workflows: A strong conceptual understanding of how engineers work (Git, CI/CD pipelines, IDE environments). You don't need to write the code, but you must speak the language.
AI & LLM Curiosity: A foundational understanding of Large Language Models and agentic workflows. You should understand the "art of the possible" regarding what AI can do for code
summary: The Senior Technical Product Manager will lead the development of Agentic IDEs, defining the product vision and roadmap to transform the engineering workflow with AI capabilities. The role requires a strong technical mindset, product discovery frameworks, and influence to drive behavioral change across the organization. The ideal candidate will have experience in product management, developer tools, and a conceptual understanding of modern dev workflows and AI.

title: AI Product Manager (m/f/x)
jd_text:
About Us
We’re
Cortea
, a Berlin startup
transforming audits with AI
. Manual, document-heavy audits waste expert time while demand keeps rising. Our
AI-powered software and specialized AI agents
remove the repetitive work so auditors can focus on judgment.
Backed by top-tier VCs with >15m funding, with a working product and paying customers, we’re rapidly scaling.
We value
first-principles thinking, speed, trust, and kindness
. We build side by side
in our Berlin office
.
Your Role
As an AI Product Manager, you'll work on products in our AI audit toolbox alongside engineers, designers, auditors and our founders. You'll spend your time understanding what auditors need, helping shape what we build next, and making sure we ship things that actually solve real problems.
What You'll Do
Contribute to the discovery, definition, and delivery of products in our AI toolbox
Talk to customers weekly to uncover pain points and validate solutions
Prototype with AI tools (Cursor, Claude, v0, etc.) to test ideas in hours, instead of weeks
Work tightly with engineering and design throughout the build cycle
Help us understand the messy reality of AI capabilities (and limits) and turn that into product decisions: what's worth building, what's not yet ready, where humans stay in the loop
Bring structure to the problem space: clear writing, sharp questions, useful PRDs
What Success Looks Like
You've contributed to and shipped 3+ meaningful product bets within your first 6 months, learning through iteration and customer feedback along the way.
You've gone deep on at least one core product area (e.g. a specific tool in our AI toolbox, a customer segment, our evals & quality framework, or the UX of agentic workflows) and become exceptionally strong in it
You're trusted by founders, engineers, and customers to steward important product decisions
Who You Are
Have 4–7 years of experience, with at least 2 years in a product role
Have shipped B2B SaaS products that real customers pay for and use
Are AI-native in how you work: you use LLMs, agents, and AI tooling daily and have strong intuition for what they can and can't do today
Have shipped at least one AI-powered feature or product and understand the unique product challenges (evals, latency, hallucinations, trust, UX of probabilistic systems)
Go deep into context to truly understand user problems
Are comfortable in early-stage startup chaos. High autonomy, high ambiguity, high speed
Bonus points
You've founded a company or shipped a serious AI side project
You can read code, run small scripts, or fine-tune prompts yourself
Experience in audit, compliance, legal, or another document-heavy professional services domain
What we offer
High impact & growth: Front-row seat to a fast-scaling AI startup, with the chance to grow alongside the product
Mission-driven culture: First-principles thinking, bold ideas, and a supportive team
Attractive compensation: competitive salary + meaningful equity
Personal development: Learning budget for courses and conferences
Dynamic environment: Central Berlin office, team lunches, retreats, flexible vacation
Interview process
First Call: Intro to Cortea with our Founders Associate Liza
Second Call: Product deep-dive with our Co-Founder & CPO Philipp
Third Call: Deep dive into our culture with our Co-Founder & CEO Valentin
On-site Day (Berlin): Meet the team and work on a real problem together
summary: The AI Product Manager role at Cortea involves working on AI-powered products for audits, collaborating with engineers, designers, and auditors to understand customer needs and ship meaningful products. The role requires strong intuition for AI capabilities, ability to go deep into context to understand user problems, and comfort in early-stage startup chaos.

Hohe Eigenmotivation und Leistungsanspruch

Karriereentwicklung: Wir unterstützen dein Wachstum in jede Richtung - ob du deine Expertise vertiefen, eine Abteilung leiten oder neue Bereiche erkunden möchtest.

title: Product Manager (all genders)

Your mission
As our first dedicated Product Manager, you'll take ownership of PartsOS, from discovery through delivery — alongside the founder who's been driving product so far. Based in Berlin, with occasional trips to see customers on site, you'll turn one of industry's most overlooked problems into software factories trust every day. What You’ll Be Working On
Own product outcomes from discovery through delivery: talk to customers, frame the real problem, decide what we build, and ship it to production.
Shape the solution end to end, from the customer problem to the interface people actually use, making a complex domain feel simple.
Get hands-on with the data and the planning logic: query it yourself, understand how our forecasting and optimization models behave, and troubleshoot when something's off.
Put AI to work on real customer problems. Weave it deep into how spare-parts planning gets done as a core part of the product, not a bolt-on.
Stay close to our customers, from a world-famous motorsport team to global leaders in industrial automation and packaging, and turn what you learn into roadmap and measurable impact.
Who We’re Looking For
Must-Have
3+ years in product management for B2B, enterprise, or technical/data-heavy products, owning outcomes from discovery to delivery.
Comfortable getting hands-on with data: querying it yourself, understanding how optimization and forecasting models work, and troubleshooting rather than treating the system as a black box.
Genuinely fluent with AI: you know how to weave it into solving real customer problems, not just how to chat with it.
A talent for simplification: you turn complex, technical concepts into clear, usable product and can shape that experience yourself.
A self-starter with customers and ambiguity: you seek out users firsthand and are energized by being the first dedicated PM rather than following a finished playbook.
Fluent in English.
Great If You Also Bring
Strong working German: a real plus for getting close to our customers.
Exposure to ERP, supply chain, manufacturing, or other complex industrial domains.
Experience in a startup or high-growth environment.
Mindset
You want real ownership, from the customer problem to what ships in production.
You love the speed and access of working directly with a founder, and you want to learn at that pace.
You'd rather build something that ships than polish a finished product from a distance.
You're not squeamish about a bit of logic and math.
You prefer working in person over coordinating from a distance.
Not Quite Your Thing?
You want a tightly scoped backlog handed to you rather than owning the discovery yourself.
You prefer to stay above the technical detail and treat the optimization as a black box.
You need a fully remote setup.
You'd rather work in a large product org with clearly defined swim lanes than be the first PM in a fast-moving team.
A bit of math and data querying doesn't sound appealing.
Why You’ll Love This Role
Work shoulder to shoulder with a founder. This is about as close to the action as product gets: fast decisions, no bureaucracy, and the experience of building something real next to the person who started it. You'll learn an enormous amount, fast.
An office-first culture in Berlin. We believe the best work happens shoulder to shoulder. You'll join a team that values in-person collaboration, fast feedback loops, and the energy of working side by side every day. The occasional home office day is fine but if you're looking for a remote-heavy setup, this isn't the right role.
Annual team week. Once a year we all come together somewhere inspiring, working, building, and enjoying each other's company.
Shared success. You'll be part of our Virtual Stock Incentive Plan (VSIP), giving you a real stake in what we're building together.
A career that grows as fast as we do. In a wide-open categor
